NewsApplication documentation¶

Models¶

This module defines the database models for the News Application. Includes content models like Article, Newsletter, and CustomUser for role-based access.

class NewsApplication.models.Article(*args, **kwargs)¶

Represents a news article submitted by a journalist. Articles must be approved by an editor before they are publicly visible.

exception DoesNotExist¶
exception MultipleObjectsReturned¶
class NewsApplication.models.CustomUser(*args, **kwargs)¶

Extended user model with role-based access control.

Supports three roles: reader, editor, and journalist. Each role has different permissions and relationships.

exception DoesNotExist¶
exception MultipleObjectsReturned¶
save(*args, **kwargs)¶

Save the user and enforce role-specific field constraints.

class NewsApplication.models.Newsletter(*args, **kwargs)¶

Represents a newsletter authored by a journalist.

exception DoesNotExist¶
exception MultipleObjectsReturned¶
class NewsApplication.models.Publisher(*args, **kwargs)¶

Represents a news publisher that can have multiple editors and journalists.

exception DoesNotExist¶
exception MultipleObjectsReturned¶
NewsApplication.models.enforce_role_field_nullification(sender, instance, created, **kwargs)¶

Ensures that if a user is a Journalist, their Reader fields are strictly empty (‘None’), and if they are a Reader, their Journalist fields are strictly empty.

Views¶

This module contains the views for rendering templates and handling HTTP requests. It includes authentication views, dashboards, and standard CRUD operations.

class NewsApplication.views.ArticleApproveView(**kwargs)¶

Approves a pending article and sends email notifications to all readers subscribed to the article’s author(s) or their publishers.

class NewsApplication.views.ArticleCreateView(**kwargs)¶

Allows journalists to create a new article draft.

form_class¶

alias of ArticleForm

form_valid(form)¶

If the form is valid, save the associated model.

model¶

alias of Article

class NewsApplication.views.ArticleDeleteView(**kwargs)¶

Allows journalists to delete one of their own articles.

get_queryset()¶

Return the QuerySet that will be used to look up the object.

This method is called by the default implementation of get_object() and may not be called if get_object() is overridden.

model¶

alias of Article

class NewsApplication.views.ArticleDetailView(**kwargs)¶

Displays the full content of a single article.

get_queryset()¶

SECURITY: - Editors can view ALL articles (so they can review drafts). - Journalists can view ALL articles (so they can read their own drafts). - Readers & Anonymous users can ONLY view approved articles.

model¶

alias of Article

class NewsApplication.views.ArticleList(**kwargs)¶

Displays a personalized article feed based on the reader’s subscriptions.

get_queryset()¶

Return the list of items for this view.

The return value must be an iterable and may be an instance of QuerySet in which case QuerySet specific behavior will be enabled.

model¶

alias of Article

class NewsApplication.views.ArticleUpdateView(**kwargs)¶

Allows journalists to edit one of their own articles.

form_class¶

alias of ArticleForm

get_queryset()¶

Return the QuerySet that will be used to look up the object.

This method is called by the default implementation of get_object() and may not be called if get_object() is overridden.

model¶

alias of Article

class NewsApplication.views.EditorDashboard(**kwargs)¶

Dashboard for editors showing articles pending approval.

class NewsApplication.views.HomeView(**kwargs)¶

Displays the landing page containing a list of approved articles. Articles are ordered from newest to oldest.

get_queryset()¶

Return the list of items for this view.

The return value must be an iterable and may be an instance of QuerySet in which case QuerySet specific behavior will be enabled.

model¶

alias of Article

class NewsApplication.views.JournalistDashboard(**kwargs)¶

Dashboard for journalists showing their articles and newsletters.

class NewsApplication.views.NewsletterCreateView(**kwargs)¶

Allows journalists to create a new newsletter.

form_class¶

alias of NewsletterForm

form_valid(form)¶

If the form is valid, save the associated model.

model¶

alias of Newsletter

class NewsApplication.views.NewsletterDeleteView(**kwargs)¶

Allows journalists to delete one of their own newsletters.

get_queryset()¶

Return the QuerySet that will be used to look up the object.

This method is called by the default implementation of get_object() and may not be called if get_object() is overridden.

model¶

alias of Newsletter

class NewsApplication.views.NewsletterUpdateView(**kwargs)¶

Allows journalists to edit one of their own newsletters.

form_class¶

alias of NewsletterForm

get_queryset()¶

Return the QuerySet that will be used to look up the object.

This method is called by the default implementation of get_object() and may not be called if get_object() is overridden.

model¶

alias of Newsletter

class NewsApplication.views.PublisherCreateView(**kwargs)¶

Allows editors to create a new publisher.

model¶

alias of Publisher

class NewsApplication.views.PublisherListView(**kwargs)¶

Lists all publishers. Only accessible to editors.

model¶

alias of Publisher

class NewsApplication.views.PublisherStaffManageView(**kwargs)¶

Allows editors to assign or remove journalists from a publisher.

form_class¶

alias of PublisherStaffForm

model¶

alias of Publisher

class NewsApplication.views.ReaderNewsletterList(**kwargs)¶

Displays newsletters from journalists the reader is subscribed to.

get_queryset()¶

Return the list of items for this view.

The return value must be an iterable and may be an instance of QuerySet in which case QuerySet specific behavior will be enabled.

model¶

alias of Newsletter

class NewsApplication.views.ReaderSubscriptionsView(**kwargs)¶

Displays and manages a reader’s journalist and publisher subscriptions.

class NewsApplication.views.RoleBasedLoginView(**kwargs)¶

Custom login view that redirects users to role-specific dashboards after successful authentication.

  • Journalists are sent to their dashboard.

  • Editors are sent to the review queue.

  • Readers are sent to their personalized article feed.

get_success_url()¶

Return the URL to redirect to after a successful login based on the user’s role.

class NewsApplication.views.SignUpView(**kwargs)¶

Handles new user registration.

Uses CustomUserRegistrationForm and redirects to the home page with a success message on completion.

form_class¶

alias of CustomUserRegistrationForm

class NewsApplication.views.SubscribedArticleFeedAPI(**kwargs)¶

REST API endpoint that returns a JSON list of approved articles from journalists the authenticated reader subscribes to.

get_queryset()¶

Get the list of items for this view. This must be an iterable, and may be a queryset. Defaults to using self.queryset.

This method should always be used rather than accessing self.queryset directly, as self.queryset gets evaluated only once, and those results are cached for all subsequent requests.

You may want to override this if you need to provide different querysets depending on the incoming request.

(Eg. return a list of items that is specific to the user)

serializer_class¶

alias of ArticleSerializer

class NewsApplication.views.ToggleJournalistSubscription(**kwargs)¶

Toggles a reader’s subscription to a specific journalist.

class NewsApplication.views.TogglePublisherSubscription(**kwargs)¶

Handles the addition and removal of Publisher entities from the Reader’s relational mapping.

Forms¶

Forms for the NewsApplication.

Provides custom forms for user registration, article creation, newsletter management, and publisher staff assignment.

class NewsApplication.forms.ArticleForm(data=None, files=None, auto_id='id_%s', prefix=None, initial=None, error_class=<class 'django.forms.utils.ErrorList'>, label_suffix=None, empty_permitted=False, instance=None, use_required_attribute=None, renderer=None)¶

Form for creating and editing articles.

property media¶

Return all media required to render the widgets on this form.

class NewsApplication.forms.CustomUserRegistrationForm(*args, **kwargs)¶

Registration form that includes username, email, and role selection.

property media¶

Return all media required to render the widgets on this form.

class NewsApplication.forms.NewsletterForm(data=None, files=None, auto_id='id_%s', prefix=None, initial=None, error_class=<class 'django.forms.utils.ErrorList'>, label_suffix=None, empty_permitted=False, instance=None, use_required_attribute=None, renderer=None)¶

Form for creating and editing newsletters.

property media¶

Return all media required to render the widgets on this form.

class NewsApplication.forms.PublisherStaffForm(*args, **kwargs)¶

Form for managing which journalists belong to a publisher.

property media¶

Return all media required to render the widgets on this form.

Serializers¶

REST framework serializers for the NewsApplication.

Provides serializers for articles and their authors for the API feed.

class NewsApplication.serializers.ArticleSerializer(*args, **kwargs)¶

Serializes an Article with nested author details.

class NewsApplication.serializers.AuthorSerializer(*args, **kwargs)¶

Serializes a CustomUser to display username and role.

Admin¶

Django admin configuration for the NewsApplication.

Registers Article, Newsletter, Publisher, and CustomUser models with the admin site and extends the UserAdmin to include the role field.

class NewsApplication.admin.CustomUserAdmin(model, admin_site)¶

Custom admin configuration for the CustomUser model.

Extends the default UserAdmin to expose the role field in both the user edit and user creation interfaces.

NewsApplication
